Your app is probably leaking requests while you read this sentence.
Not “might be”. Probably.
I have run the five checks below on eleven production Angular apps over the past two years, apps built by good teams at real companies. Ten of the eleven failed at least one check. The teams were always surprised. The network tab never was.
Here is the thing about leaking HTTP requests: they do not throw errors. They do not appear in Sentry. They do not fail a single test. They just quietly burn bandwidth, hammer your backend, corrupt your UI with stale data, and grow your memory footprint, while every dashboard stays green.
So this article is not a lecture about fixes. It is a diagnostic. Five signs, each with the exact steps to check your own app in under two minutes, and a verdict at the end. Open your app in a second tab right now. By the end of this read you will know, with evidence, whether your app is one of the ten or the one.
Let us examine the patient.
Sign 1: Requests That Keep Arriving After You Leave the Page
The dead giveaway, and the first check I run on any codebase.
